ideas.title: day_length_delta() pure fn — 'today is N min longer/shorter than yesterday'
ideas.why: coverage_fns=10 are all astronomy math and wx.json already carries sunrise/sunset, so a delta fn is a naturally testable extension of the 100%-gated blog-logic crate.

ideas.title: Respect prefers-reduced-motion on the SunArc + sky-gradient animations
ideas.why: the sky background and SunArc animate continuously with no reduced-motion guard, a self-contained CSS media-query tweak that improves a11y without touching logic.

ideas.title: golden_hour() pure fn — compute golden/blue-hour windows from sunrise/sunset in wx.json
ideas.why: wx.json's astronomy block gives sunrise/sunset but the UI only shows raw times, and a golden-hour offset is deterministic math that slots straight into the 100%-coverage gate.

ideas.title: Trim wasm bundle toward a budget check — opt-level=z + wasm-opt in the gate, flag regressions
ideas.why: wasm_kb=481 with 147 commits/7d means bundle size drifts silently every deploy, and a size-budget assertion (fail/ warn over a threshold) is a small reversible CI guard.

ideas.title: MOONSHOT: browser-side WASM playground that runs blog-logic fns live (astronomy calculator you can drag)
ideas.why: blog-logic is a pure Rust crate already compiled to WASM and 100%-tested, so exposing its 10 fns behind interactive sliders (move the sun, watch day_length/golden_hour recompute) is a rare 'the site runs its own tested code' showpiece.

ideas.title: MOONSHOT: WebGL 'living sky' hero — real-time sun/moon/star field driven by wx.json + local time
ideas.why: we already fetch same-origin astronomy in wx.json and render a flat gradient, so a GPU shader sky (sun arc, atmospheric scattering by hour, stars at night, current-condition clouds/rain) turns existing data into a whoa-grade hero.
